 {"id":"bd-1syw","title":"Task: Register agents with Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Purpose
Automatically register each spawned agent with Agent Mail for inter-agent communication.

## Scope
- On spawn, call register_agent for each non-user pane
- Use auto-generated adjective+noun names per Agent Mail conventions
- Store pane -> agent name mapping for session
- Create project-wide coordination thread

## Registration Flow
for _, pane := range panes {
    if pane.Type == AgentUser { continue }
    
    agent, err := am.RegisterAgent(ctx, RegisterAgentOptions{
        ProjectKey:      s.projectDir,
        Program:         'ntm',
        Model:           pane.Variant,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf('Agent in pane %s', pane.Title),
    })
    
    s.agentRegistry[pane.ID] = agent.Name
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents registered with unique names
- Mapping stored for message routing
- Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- Registration survives session restart

## Technical Notes
Reuse existing agentmail client
Store mapping in session state","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1tb8","title":"Task: Add automatic model management for local LLMs","description":"## Overview
Enable NTM to automatically pull and manage local LLM models when needed.

## Deliverables
1. Model management commands:
   - ntm models list - show available local models
   - ntm models pull <model> - download model from Ollama registry
   - ntm models remove <model> - delete model to free space
   - ntm models recommend - suggest models based on hardware

2. Automatic pull on spawn:
   - If --local-model not found locally, offer to pull
   - Show download progress in terminal
   - Continue spawn after model ready

3. Model recommendations:
   Based on available VRAM:
   - <4GB: codellama:7b-instruct, deepseek-coder:6.7b
   - 4-8GB: codellama:13b, deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct
   - 8-16GB: codellama:34b, deepseek-coder:33b
   - 16GB+: mixtral:8x7b, codellama:70b

4. Storage management:
   - Show model sizes and disk usage
   - Warn when disk space low
   - LRU cleanup suggestion for unused models

## Implementation Notes
- Use Ollama API for all model operations
- Cache model metadata locally for fast lookup
- Support offline operation with cached models

## Acceptance Criteria
- Model commands work correctly
- Automatic pull flows smoothly
- Recommendations match hardware
- Disk space warnings appear appropriately
- Unit tests for model management","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.066483753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:45.145324634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.095132754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:45.145289538Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location

- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

 {"id":"bd-1tu58","title":"Unit Tests: ntm spawn --assign flag integration","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the spawn --assign convenience flag integration.

## Test Cases

### Flag Parsing
- Parse --assign flag correctly
- Parse --assign with --strategy flag
- Parse --assign with --limit flag
- Error when --assign used without agents

### Integration with ntm assign
- Spawn --assign calls ntm assign internally
- Strategy flag passed through correctly
- Limit flag passed through correctly
- Agent filters applied correctly

### Combined Output
- Output includes spawn result
- Output includes assign result
- JSON format consistent with robot mode

### Error Handling
- Handle assign failure after successful spawn
- Handle no beads available
- Handle all agents busy

## Logging Requirements
- Log spawn phase completion
- Log assign invocation with parameters
- Log combined result

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-1tv6","title":"Task: Implement assignment algorithm","description":"## Purpose
Match beads to agents using capability scores and availability.

## Algorithm
func assignTasks(beads []Bead, agents []Agent, strategy string) []Assignment

For each bead:
1. Filter agents by availability (idle, sufficient context)
2. Score each agent: capability_score * (1 - context_usage)
3. Apply strategy modifiers:
   - balanced: spread work evenly
   - speed: assign to any available
   - quality: assign to highest scorer
   - dependency: prioritize blockers

## Assignment Struct
type Assignment struct {
    Bead       Bead
    Agent      Agent
    Score      float64
    Reason     string
    Confidence float64
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Optimal assignments based on strategy
- Handles more beads than agents
- Handles more agents than beads
- Provides reasoning for each assignment

## Technical Notes
Consider Hungarian algorithm for optimal matching
Start with greedy approach for simplicity","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:21.840916046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.782032302Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.781936402Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tv6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3b3f","title":"Unit Tests: TUI rendering correctness and regression prevention","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit test suite for TUI rendering to prevent regressions and verify fixes for UI glitches.

## Test File Location
internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard_render_test.go

## Test Categories

### 1. View Height Management Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_OutputFitsTerminal(t *testing.T) {
    sizes := []struct{ w, h int }{
        {80, 24},   // Minimum
        {120, 40},  // Standard
        {200, 60},  // Large
    }
    for _, sz := range sizes {
        t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%dx%d", sz.w, sz.h), func(t *testing.T) {
            m := NewModel(testSession)
            m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: sz.w, Height: sz.h})
            
            view := m.View()
            lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
            
            if len(lines) > sz.h {
                t.Errorf("view has %d lines, terminal height is %d", len(lines), sz.h)
            }
        })
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Single-Render Tests (No Duplication)
\`\`\`go
func TestView_StatusBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "Fleet:")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'Fleet:', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_HelpBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "navigate")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'navigate', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_NoDuplicationOnResize(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    
    // Resize multiple times
    for _, h := range []int{24, 40, 20, 60, 30} {
        m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: h})
        view := m.View()
        
        if strings.Count(view, "Fleet:") != 1 {
            t.Errorf("height %d: status bar duplicated", h)
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Width Calculation Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_WidthCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify we use lipgloss.Width not len()
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("test")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("styled 'test' should have visual width 4, got %d", lipgloss.Width(styled))
    }
    
    if len(styled) <= 4 {
        t.Error("styled string should be longer than 4 bytes (includes ANSI)")
    }
}

func TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation(t *testing.T) {
    hints := []components.KeyHint{
        {Key: "↑↓", Desc: "navigate"},
        {Key: "1-9", Desc: "select"},
        {Key: "z", Desc: "zoom"},
        {Key: "r", Desc: "refresh"},
        {Key: "?", Desc: "help"},
        {Key: "q", Desc: "quit"},
    }
    
    // Narrow width should truncate
    narrow := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 40,
    })
    
    // Wide width should show all
    wide := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 200,
    })
    
    if lipgloss.Width(narrow) > 40 {
        t.Errorf("narrow help bar exceeds width: %d > 40", lipgloss.Width(narrow))
    }
    
    if !strings.Contains(wide, "quit") {
        t.Error("wide help bar should contain 'quit'")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 4. List Deduplication Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with duplicates
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    if strings.Count(view, "Task A") != 1 {
        t.Error("duplicate 'Task A' found in view")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 5. State Consistency Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestModel_FleetCountConsistency(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(10)
    m.claudeCount = 5
    m.codexCount = 3
    m.geminiCount = 2
    
    // Total should match
    total := m.claudeCount + m.codexCount + m.geminiCount
    if total != len(m.panes) {
        t.Errorf("agent counts (%d) != pane count (%d)", total, len(m.panes))
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 6. Golden File Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_GoldenRender(t *testing.T) {
    m := fixtureModel()
    m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: 40})
    
    got := m.View()
    golden := filepath.Join("testdata", "dashboard_120x40.golden")
    
    if os.Getenv("UPDATE_GOLDEN") != "" {
        os.WriteFile(golden, []byte(got), 0644)
        t.Skip("golden file updated")
    }
    
    want, err := os.ReadFile(golden)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("read golden: %v", err)
    }
    
    if got != string(want) {
        t.Error("render differs from golden - run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 to update")
        // Write diff for debugging
        os.WriteFile("testdata/dashboard_120x40.got", []byte(got), 0644)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Logging in Tests
All tests should use t.Log for detailed output:
\`\`\`go
t.Logf("Terminal size: %dx%d", width, height)
t.Logf("View lines: %d", len(lines))
t.Logf("Status bar count: %d", statusCount)
\`\`\`

## Test Data Fixtures
Create testdata/fixtures.go with:
- fixtureModel() - standard model with realistic data
- modelWithPanes(n) - model with n panes
- modelWithStatus(statuses) - model with specific statuses

## Coverage Requirements
- Minimum 80% coverage on dashboard.go View()
- 100% coverage on components/help.go RenderHelpBar()
- 90% coverage on layout calculations

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
test-tui:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - run: go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/tui/...
    - run: go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep -E "View|Render"

 {"id":"bd-3b4yu","title":"Add ntm assign --watch for continuous auto-assignment on completion","description":"## Overview
Continuous auto-assignment mode that watches for completions and assigns new work automatically.

## Use Cases
1. "Fire and forget" swarm operation
2. Long-running development sessions
3. Overnight batch processing

## Usage
```bash
# Watch and auto-assign with default strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch

# With specific strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch --strategy=dependency

# With limit per cycle
ntm assign myproject --watch --limit=2

# Stop when no more beads
ntm assign myproject --watch --stop-when-done

# With delay between assignments
ntm assign myproject --watch --delay=5s
```

## Architecture
```go
type WatchLoop struct {
    session         string
    strategy        Strategy
    store           *AssignmentStore
    detector        *CompletionDetector
    depAssigner     *DependencyAwareAssigner
    
    // Concurrency control
    completionCh    chan CompletionEvent
    assignmentLock  sync.Mutex
    stopCh          chan struct{}
    wg              sync.WaitGroup
}
```

## How It Works
1. Initial assignment pass (same as ntm assign --auto)
2. Start completion detector in separate goroutine
3. Enter main loop:
   a. Wait for completion event on channel
   b. Lock assignment mutex
   c. Update assignment store
   d. Check for newly unblocked beads
   e. Assign to idle agents
   f. Unlock mutex
   g. Continue loop
4. Exit conditions:
   - User interrupt (Ctrl+C)
   - --stop-when-done and no more ready beads
   - All agents failed/crashed

## Concurrency Model
```
┌─────────────────────┐
│   Main Goroutine    │
│  (watch loop)       │
└─────────┬───────────┘
          │ reads from
          ▼
   completionCh
          ▲
          │ writes to
┌─────────┴───────────┐
│  Detector Goroutine │
│  (polls status,     │
│   matches patterns) │
└─────────────────────┘

Critical section: All assignment operations protected by assignmentLock
```

## Serialized Completion Handling
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) run(ctx context.Context) {
    for {
        select {
        case event := <-w.completionCh:
            w.assignmentLock.Lock()
            
            // 1. Update store
            w.store.MarkCompleted(event.BeadID)
            
            // 2. Log completion
            w.logCompletion(event)
            
            // 3. Check unblocked
            unblocked := w.depAssigner.GetNewlyUnblocked()
            
            // 4. Assign to idle
            idle := w.store.GetIdleAgents()
            w.assignNextBeads(unblocked, idle)
            
            w.assignmentLock.Unlock()
            
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-w.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}
```

## Output (Streaming)
```
[12:34:01] Starting watch mode with strategy=balanced
[12:34:01] Initial assignment: 4 beads to 4 agents
[12:34:02]   bd-abc1 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
[12:34:02]   bd-def2 -> pane 3 (BlueLake)
[12:38:45] Completion: bd-abc1 by GreenCastle (4m 43s)
[12:38:45] Unblocked: bd-mno5, bd-pqr6
[12:38:46] Assigned: bd-mno5 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
...
[13:45:00] All beads complete. Exiting watch mode.
```

## Graceful Shutdown
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) Shutdown() {
    // 1. Signal stop
    close(w.stopCh)
    
    // 2. Wait for in-flight completions
    w.wg.Wait()
    
    // 3. Don't assign new work, but let current finish
    w.detector.Stop()
    
    // 4. Save state for resume
    w.store.Save()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watches for completion events
- [ ] Auto-assigns on completion
- [ ] Respects strategy flag
- [ ] --stop-when-done exits cleanly
- [ ] --delay controls assignment pacing
- [ ] Graceful shutdown on interrupt
- [ ] Streaming output with timestamps
- [ ] Handles all agents crashing
- [ ] Thread-safe completion handling
- [ ] No race conditions (passes go test -race)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Watch loop logic
- Unit test: Exit conditions
- Unit test: Concurrent completions (10+ goroutines)
- E2E test: Full watch cycle with completions
- Race test: go test -race flag","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:43.182014392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:44.791548718Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:44.791463197Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete:

      "usage_percent": 45,
      "limit_reset": "2024-01-15T12:00:00Z",
      "available_accounts": 3
    },
    "openai": {
      "current": "account2@email.com",
      "usage_percent": 80,
      "limit_reset": "2024-01-15T11:30:00Z",
      "available_accounts": 2

```

## Command: --robot-accounts-list

```bash
ntm --robot-accounts-list
ntm --robot-accounts-list --provider claude
```

```json

  "accounts": [
    {
      "provider": "claude",
      "email": "account1@email.com",
      "current": true,
      "usage_percent": 45,
      "cooldown": null
    },
    {
      "provider": "claude",

      "current": false,
      "usage_percent": 0,
      "cooldown": "2024-01-15T11:00:00Z"

  ]
}
```

## Implementation
Both commands invoke caam with --json flag and transform output.

 {"id":"bd-3btcc","title":"Subtask: Unit Tests for --robot-is-working Command","description":"## Subtask: Unit Tests for --robot-is-working Command

### Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for the is-working command, including parser integration, output formatting, summary generation, and edge cases.

### Test File
`internal/robot/isworking_test.go`

### Test Coverage

```go
package robot

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "testing"

    "github.com/username/ntm/internal/agent"
)

func TestIsWorking_SinglePane_Working(t *testing.T) {
    // Mock capturePaneOutput to return working agent output
    mockOutput := `Opus 4.5 · Claude Max · Personal

Let me create that function for you.

` + "```go\n" + `
func hello() {
    fmt.Println("Hello")
}
` + "```"

    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{2}, mockOutput)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    pane, ok := result.Panes["2"]
    if !ok {
        t.Fatal("Expected pane 2 in results")
    }

    if !pane.IsWorking {
        t.Error("Expected IsWorking=true for agent writing code")
    }
    if pane.Recommendation != "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT" {
        t.Errorf("Expected DO_NOT_INTERRUPT, got %s", pane.Recommendation)
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_SinglePane_Idle(t *testing.T) {
    mockOutput := `47% context left · ? for shortcuts

>`

    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{3}, mockOutput)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    pane := result.Panes["3"]
    if pane.IsWorking {
        t.Error("Expected IsWorking=false for idle agent")
    }
    if !pane.IsIdle {
        t.Error("Expected IsIdle=true")
    }
    if pane.Recommendation != "SAFE_TO_RESTART" {
        t.Errorf("Expected SAFE_TO_RESTART, got %s", pane.Recommendation)
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_SinglePane_RateLimited(t *testing.T) {
    mockOutput := `You've hit your limit for today. Please wait until tomorrow.`

    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{4}, mockOutput)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    pane := result.Panes["4"]
    if !pane.IsRateLimited {
        t.Error("Expected IsRateLimited=true")
    }
    if pane.Recommendation != "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT" {
        t.Errorf("Expected RATE_LIMITED_WAIT, got %s", pane.Recommendation)
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_ContextExtraction_Codex(t *testing.T) {
    mockOutput := `47% context left · ? for shortcuts

>`

    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{3}, mockOutput)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    pane := result.Panes["3"]
    if pane.ContextRemaining == nil {
        t.Fatal("Expected ContextRemaining to be set for Codex")
    }
    if *pane.ContextRemaining != 47.0 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 47.0%%, got %.1f%%", *pane.ContextRemaining)
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_LowContext_Flag(t *testing.T) {
    mockOutput := `12% context left · ? for shortcuts

>`

    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{3}, mockOutput)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed: %v", err)
    }

    pane := result.Panes["3"]
    if !pane.IsContextLow {
        t.Error("Expected IsContextLow=true for 12%")
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_MultiplePanes(t *testing.T) {
    // Simulate multiple panes with different states
    result := &IsWorkingResult{
        Panes: map[string]PaneWorkStatus{
            "2": {IsWorking: true, Recommendation: "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT"},
            "3": {IsIdle: true, Recommendation: "SAFE_TO_RESTART"},
            "4": {IsRateLimited: true, Recommendation: "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT"},
        },
    }

    summary := buildSummary(result.Panes)

    if summary.TotalPanes != 3 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 3 total panes, got %d", summary.TotalPanes)
    }
    if summary.WorkingCount != 1 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 1 working, got %d", summary.WorkingCount)
    }
    if summary.IdleCount != 1 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 1 idle, got %d", summary.IdleCount)
    }
    if summary.RateLimitedCount != 1 {
        t.Errorf("Expected 1 rate limited, got %d", summary.RateLimitedCount)
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_JSONOutput(t *testing.T) {
    result := &IsWorkingResult{
        Session:   "test",
        Timestamp: "2026-01-20T15:30:00Z",
        Panes: map[string]PaneWorkStatus{
            "2": {AgentType: "cc", IsWorking: true},
        },
    }

    data, err := json.Marshal(result)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("JSON marshal failed: %v", err)
    }

    // Verify it's valid JSON
    var parsed map[string]interface{}
    if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &parsed); err != nil {
        t.Errorf("Output is not valid JSON: %v", err)
    }

    // Verify required fields
    if _, ok := parsed["session"]; !ok {
        t.Error("Missing 'session' in output")
    }
    if _, ok := parsed["panes"]; !ok {
        t.Error("Missing 'panes' in output")
    }
}

func TestIsWorking_EmptyPaneOutput(t *testing.T) {
    result, err := testIsWorking("test_session", []int{2}, "")
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("IsWorking failed on empty: %v", err)
    }

    pane := result.Panes["2"]
    if pane.AgentType != "unknown" {
        t.Errorf("Expected unknown agent type for empty output, got %s", pane.AgentType)
    }
    if pane.Confidence > 0.5 {
        t.Errorf("Expected low confidence for empty output, got %.2f", pane.Confidence)
    }
}

func TestRecommendationReason(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        state    *agent.AgentState
        contains string
    }{
        {
            &agent.AgentState{IsWorking: true},
            "actively",
        },
        {
            &agent.AgentState{IsIdle: true},
            "idle",
        },
        {
            &agent.AgentState{IsRateLimited: true},
            "rate limit",
        },
        {
            &agent.AgentState{IsWorking: true, IsContextLow: true, ContextRemaining: ptr(15.0)},
            "low context",
        },
    }

    for i, tt := range tests {
        reason := getRecommendationReason(tt.state)
        if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(reason), tt.contains) {
            t.Errorf("Test %d: reason %q should contain %q", i, reason, tt.contains)
        }
    }
}

// Helper for tests
func testIsWorking(session string, panes []int, mockOutput string) (*IsWorkingResult, error) {
    // Set up mock
    originalCapture := capturePaneOutput
    capturePaneOutput = func(s string, p int, lines int) (string, error) {
        return mockOutput, nil
    }
    defer func() { capturePaneOutput = originalCapture }()

    return IsWorking(session, panes, false)
}

func ptr(f float64) *float64 { return &f }
```

### Test Logging

```go
// Each test should log key decisions for debugging
func TestIsWorking_DetailedLogging(t *testing.T) {
    // Enable verbose logging for this test
    t.Log("Testing is-working with verbose output")

    mockOutput := `47% context left`
    result, _ := testIsWorking("test", []int{2}, mockOutput)

    pane := result.Panes["2"]
    t.Logf("Agent type detected: %s", pane.AgentType)
    t.Logf("Context remaining: %v", pane.ContextRemaining)
    t.Logf("Recommendation: %s", pane.Recommendation)
    t.Logf("Confidence: %.2f", pane.Confidence)
}
```

- assigned -> failed
- Clear assignment on pane kill
- Handle duplicate assignments

Each test should log state before/after operations with timestamps.
